sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 15.48 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.15)

statement.latex: Let$G$be any non-trivial finite group, and let$X$be any generating set for$G$. Is it true that every element of$G$can be obtained from$X$using fewer than$2 _2|G|$multiplications? (When counting the number of mutiplications on a path from the generators to a given element, at each step one can use the elements obtained at previous steps.)

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
